formulas.name: Content
formulas.formula:
Here's a comprehensive formula sheet for CBSE Class 11 Computer Science - Getting Started with Python:

```json
[
  {
    "name": "Input/Output Operations",
    "formula": "print() - Prints the output to the screen",
    "variables": "expression - The value to be printed",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use print() function to display output on the screen."
  },
  {
    "name": "Input/Output Operations",
    "formula": "input() - Takes user input from the keyboard",
    "variables": "expression - The value to be taken as input",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use input() function to take user input from the keyboard."
  },
  {
    "name": "Data Types",
    "formula": "int() - Converts a number to an integer",
    "variables": "number - The number to be converted",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use int() function to convert a number to an integer."
  },
  {
    "name": "Data Types",
    "formula": "float() - Converts a number to a floating point number",
    "variables": "number - The number to be converted",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use float() function to convert a number to a floating point number."
  },
  {
    "name": "Data Types",
    "formula": "str() - Converts a string to a string",
    "variables": "string - The string to be converted",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use str() function to convert a string to a string."
  },
  {
    "name": "Data Types",
    "formula": "bool() - Converts a value to a boolean",
    "variables": "value - The value to be converted",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use bool() function to convert a value to a boolean."
  },
  {
    "name": "Basic Operators",
    "formula": "Arithmetic Operators: +, -, \*, /, %, **",
    "variables": "a, b - The numbers to be operated on",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use arithmetic operators to perform arithmetic operations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Basic Operators",
    "formula": "Comparison Operators: ==, !=, <, >, <=, >=",
    "variables": "a, b - The numbers to be compared",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use comparison operators to compare values."
  },
  {
    "name": "Basic Operators",
    "formula": "Logical Operators: and, or, not",
    "variables": "a, b - The boolean values to be operated on",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use logical operators to perform logical operations."
  },
  {
    "name": "Control Structures",
    "formula": "if-else Statement: if condition: statement else: statement",
    "variables": "condition - The condition to be evaluated",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use if-else statement to execute different blocks of code based on a condition."
  },
  {
    "name": "Control Structures",
    "formula": "for Loop: for variable in iterable: statement",
    "variables": "variable - The variable to be iterated over",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use for loop to iterate over a sequence."
  },
  {
    "name": "Control Structures",
    "formula": "while Loop: while condition: statement",
    "variables": "condition - The condition to be evaluated",
    "units": "",
    "notes": "Use while loop to repeat a block of code while a condition is true."
  }
]
```

Note: Since Python is a programming language and not a mathematical subject, there are no mathematical formulas in this chapter. However, I have included key statements and laws related to programming concepts in the above JSON array.
